hypothesis: If the Granite 3.3 2B model is continually pre-trained on MOF-specific PMC articles combined with PubChem structural datasets and then fine-tuned on MOFid-to-property mappings using MOFid text representations, it will achieve lower mean absolute error on band gap and gas adsorption predictions than baseline models (CGCNN, SOAP, MOFormer) and retain superior performance under limited fine-tuning data.
